RESEARCH



I. What We’re Looking For…



Insights Into Demographic : 

Who Is Your Customer and their Age?

New people in E-Commerce, Home Business, Entrepreneurship over 22

What Attitudes Do They Have? (Religious, Political, Social, Economic)?

Few attitudes i noticed, motivational, social

What Are Their Hopes and Dreams?

To scale their business

What Are Their Victories and Failures?

Victories are - Lots of traffic, Allows people to shop online, attractive domain name, search engine optimization for site

Failures are - No sales, Lack of marketing, selling the wrong product online, poor checkout procedures, poor customer service, infrequent updating of website, No guest checkout

What Outside Forces Do THEY Believe Have Prevented Their Best Life?

Customer service are part of the biggest problems

What Are Their Prejudices?

“prefer to sell on Marketplaces not on my own web store”

“I invest in e-commerce only if I make money”

Sum Up Their Core Beliefs About Life, Love, and Family In 1-3 Sentences. 

Ability to maintain customer loyalty is much more important than. Returns and refunds are part of it.



Other Existing Solutions: 

What is the Market Already Using? (List Out)

SHOPIFY

BIGCommerce 

Volusion 

Storefront, by Awesome Commerce, LLC

What Has Their Experience Been Like?

A lot of trial and error

What Does the Market Like About Existing Solutions?

Able to shop mobilly 

able to work with an expert

can offer free shipping

What Does the Market Dislike About Existing Solutions?

If you don’t use online identity verification, it’s difficult to tell the difference.

Flow of inventory data from ERP to OMS to webshop

Not enough IT resources to do everything you want frontend and backend

Shopify doesn't alert you when your stock is running low

Are Their Horror Stories About Existing Solutions?

Market feels it's impossible to get any traffic to their website after spending tons of time and money - Losing motivation

Shopify inventory can be extremely difficult to navigate

App doesn't open

Does the Market Believe Existing Solution Works?

As the system doesn't give out alerts on how the order are coming along, you still have to incorporate your spreadsheets to make accurate business decisions







Curiosity: Important because it keeps you from being a worker bee and it’s powerful in copy because it peaks interest which increases engagement effectiveness

Has Someone Tried to Solve the Market’s Pain Points Before In A Very Unique Way? 

Inventory management software was made easy to use

What Was The Result?

Orders get delayed

overloaded with spreadsheets

inefficient inventory





Corruption:

Is There A Belief That the Market’s Pain Point Used To Not Exist, Or Used To Not Be So Bad?



Is There A Belief That It’s Been Recently Exacerbated By Outside Forces?



If So, What Are Those Forces And What’s The Reason Behind Their Presence?





II. Where to Find The Answers to Our Research Questions?



Forums/Reddit: 

Look for replies vs. views. Why? On a post, if lots of people view it that’s great, and there may be interest. But when there are a ton of REPLIES, it means people are really passionate, interested, and engaged.  



Examples:

https://www.reddit.com/r/fatpeoplestories/comments/28nuy3/a_rant_on_the_sad_reality_that_is_often_type_2/

https://www.reddit.com/r/fatpeoplestories/

https://www.reddit.com/r/keto/

https://bitcointalk.org/

http://www.personaldefenseforum.net/showthread.php?814-Venezuela-A-Case-for-Prepping





Facebook Groups and Pages:

Similar to forums. Pay attention to the posts with the most engagement. Not interested in a ton of “likes.” Only semi-interested in a ton of “shares.” Really interested in a ton of comments and arguments. 



Example:

https://www.facebook.com/Diabetes-Support-119703998103102/?ref=br_rs





https://www.facebook.com/pg/getkailo/reviews/



Patty Barbour - 



What a godsend! These really work great! My parents and I have been living with pain of various joints for years. I ordered these and we are so happy with the pain relief that they provide that I ordered more so we can put them on more than one place at a time. - Patty Barbour - What a godsend!



The ones I got work great. I have a spinal cord stimulated and have had many surgeries on several joints and I have fibromyalgia. I honestly couldn’t believe that these deals helped but they do. I ordered more. My dad has trouble with arthritis and these have helped him and my mom a lot.



Haley Dickey  recommends Kailo.

March 7 · 

Simply amazing!! My husband injured his back while serving in the Marine Corps and nothing has been able to help his pain. This patch works miracles. Would recommend a million times. Thank you Kailo for helping him



This tape helps me sleep without pain. I was initially skeptical as I’ve tried almost everything but I’m very grateful for it.



YouTube:

Same thing again. I’d look for one that has a good amount of views. But then I’d also look at the engagement and comments. 



Examples:

https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=bitcoin+conspiracy&sp=CAM%253D

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h8iBKbubCxM





Comment Sections on Articles: 

And same thing again. 



Examples:

http://www.breitbart.com/

http://www.breitbart.com/london/2018/04/17/macron-europe-migration-destiny-africa/

https://conservativetribune.com/





Authority Figures / Influencers:

Has Dr. Oz/Kim Kardashian/Oprah/Sanjay Gupta/Hot Lifestyle Model/Mommy Blogger/Whoever been discussing a specific pain point or solution? 



We laugh at Dr. Oz and the free trial guys (and straight sale guys too) have kind of turned it into a joke. But at the end of the day if Dr. Oz is talking about an ingredient or a product, then most baby-boomers believe It must be the greatest thing since sliced bread. 



When looking at an authority figure or influencer’s website, article, Instagram post, blog, etc…

Did they explain any science behind how the problem works, or how the product acts as a solution? 



Google:

It sounds simple but Googling the problem and solution is the best way to find information. Look at information from both Big Name sites and smaller ones. If you find something interesting, open a new tab, and start doing more research about it…



“How to”

“How does”

“What is”

“Best ways to”

“Natural ways to”

“Why do”

“____ Benefits”



For Nutra: 

Google: “ingredient name, humans, ncbi”. 

Look for double blind studies and pay attention to dosages in there, and if the ingredients in your product match or exceed those dosages. 

“Meta-Analysis” articles are super useful too.

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5664031/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4609100/

More Places to Go…

Examine.com  (Go To Human Effects Matrix For Studies)

DrAxe.com

WhFoods.com

OrganicFacts.net





Amazon.Com

No surprise here of course. But perfect for looking at similar products on the market, and reading reviews of them too. This works for both indirectly compete products, and direct competitors. 

Look at 5 Star Reviews and 1 Star Reviews. 

Look at the FAQs as this will help know what your market is wondering about. 

Look at similar products customers have bought.  

Look at product features/benefits/claims. 

https://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/B07Y3SZZLK

I learned about the Kailo through a dear friend who recommended that I try it in lieu of OTC meds and Flexaril muscle relaxer. It arrived on Monday afternoon. 5.0 out of 5 stars

 Definitely worked for me

Reviewed in the United States on November 3, 2019

Size: Pack of 1

I actually bought this product on indiegogo and just came to Amazon for the adhesive refills but I wanted to leave a review because it is that good. I have had chronic back pain for 4 years. I've been on meds for a year and a half after exhausting all other options. Because of the current climate regarding opioids my doc now wants me to stop taking my meds...with no other pain relief option offered. So I bought this patch. IT IS AMAZING. Nothing else has worked for me but this significantly reduced my pain. It does not completely removed the pain so don't expect that. But it reduced it enough that I can play with my kids, mow the lawn, work, essentially be a normal human being without medication. The biggest improvement has been to my sleep. The pain usually wakes me at night but hasn't since starting to wear this. It is large and it is stiff as another review mentioned so it's kind of hard to position comfortably. Also, the adhesive doesn't work very well if it's on a place you sweat. Despite that, I wholeheartedly recommend this product if you are in pain... It helps.

5.0 out of 5 stars

 100% No more pain!

Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2020

Size: Pack of 1Verified Purchase

I purchased this for my husband. Perspective: He has severe arthritis in both shoulders and can’t lift his hands above his head or behind his back without extreme amounts of pain. If he ever had to be handcuffed, the amount of pain would probably cause him to vomit.



I placed one patch on his left shoulder last night before bed. The adhesive works really well. He did not experience any tingling or warming so he was skeptical. When he woke up, I asked how he felt. He does not usually sleep well because the amount of pain in his shoulders. He can’t usually sleep on his sides because of this and rolling over is difficult.



He said, “This is the first night I ever slept without pain in years.” He was still oddly skeptical because he was confused. He said there’s only one patch on, but he felt no pain in both shoulders. He can lift his hands above his head and feels no pain.



I asked if it’s 50% better or 80% better, give me an idea of what it’s like? He said 100% no pain. So, I would say this product works really well after the first 10 hours. Five stars indeed. I just hope we get several months out of one patch to justify the cost. The website says they can last for years so fingers crossed!

III. IMPORTANT Notes: 



1. Through your research you’ll typically end up getting the kernel for the mechanism behind the problem and the solution. 



2. If you find a unique solution to the problem, and your product fits it, don’t be afraid to fit the two together. 



For example:



If you have a weight loss product…

And your product is geared towards helping with metabolism…

You find a lot of research about how metabolism is governed by the thyroid hormone…

You may want to bridge-the-gap, and have your product talk about…

Which means making your copy about how a dysfunctional thyroid is the real cause of your poor metabolism. 



3. Rabbit holes are your friend (within reason). I’ve found some of my most interesting unique mechanisms and my best stories by going down rabbit holes. 



Examples:



1. Maybe you’re looking at doing a DIY solar generator. You see someone’s name as an early inventor of a prototype. You look into the prototype and find out it had a novel mechanism of action. That’s all great fodder for copy. 



2. Recently writing a new offer for foot fungus. It has an ingredient called undecylenic acid in it. End up finding a U.S. Military Medical History about how during WWII, U.S. soldiers were PLAGUED by foot fungus. It was so bad it caused almost 10% of our soldiers to miss time in action. The U.S. Surgeon general at the time went on a desperate quest to find something that would control fungus. Ultimately they discovered that undecylenic acid was superior to all the rest.

"Grandma, why did they abandon me?"

I heard my 5 year-old granddaughter's voice shaking...

Well, I knew this question would come one day or the other, given that...

Little Lily was basically raised by my husband and I for the past 2 years...

You see, her father and my daughter weren't getting along, and Lily wasn't even 1 year old when they split up.

My daughter moved back in with us, and we never heard from her ex-boyfriend since...

She struggled with having the baby, and maintaining her career as a journalist, so...

About 2 years ago she got a job offer in LA she could not refuse, so...

My hubby and I offered to take care of Lily for a few months...

Two months became six, then a year, and now it's been two.

My daughter tries her best to visit us often, but she's not here for Lily most of the time...

And, to be honest, I can't imagine our life without Lily in it, she is like a second daughter to us....

Anyways, the question was asked and I told Lily the truth about what happened and that...

It has nothing to do with her, just the way life happened.

And that her mother loves her, and I love her and grandpa loves her more than anything.

That day I learned that I'm a bad child psychologist....

Lily became distant, closed and irritated.

I could see that she was angry, but every time I tried bringing up the subject, she'd close even more...

I decided to bring this up during my weekly lunch with girlfriends.

After we laughed a bit, we each started sharing how we are dealing with our grand kids' moods.

This is when Janet told me she had something that most certainly would help.

She claimed she had a book that would calm her grandson every time.

It's called "Zen Pig: The Art of Gratitude", and...

It teaches kids to live in the moment and enjoy what they have.

Janet gave me the book before the evening, so it was just in time for a bed time story.

One thing I know for sure - Lily absolutely loved the Zen Pig!

She's all forgiving and kindness now...

She learns how to find joy in little things, and appreciates what she has.

She's been happy to help me with anything at home, and we'd always connect it to one of the lessons in the book.

I can't thank my husband's co-worker enough for recommending this book.

I’m beyond grateful to the man who wrote it.

I hope you and your families are safe in this time of uncertainty, and...

I 100% suggest purchasing a copy of this book (or even the whole series)."

- Margaret Brown (Tampa, FL)
